README line 26 states '12 meal entries (6 meal kit, 6 grocery)' and setup_task.sh CSV contains exactly 6 MealKit rows and 6 Grocery rows

README line 107-108 claims 'Meal kit waste percentages are higher (15-35%)' but setup_task.sh CSV shows MealKit waste of 5%, 5%, 8%, 5%, 7%, 5% (max 8%)
README line 107-108 claims grocery waste (15-35%) and setup_task.sh CSV shows Grocery waste of 30%, 25%, 22%, 18%, 35%, 28%
README line 75 claims ~$7.50-$8.50. Calculated from data: MealKit avg = $6.70/serving base, plus $9.99/12 servings = $0.83/serving, total = $7.53 (matches range)
README line 76 claims ~$6.50-$7.50 after waste adjustment. Calculated: Grocery waste-adjusted average = $7.04 (within claimed range)

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic meal cost analysis task with 12 meal entries (6 meal kit, 6 grocery) plus subscription fee data. Verified facts: medium difficulty, 180-second timeout (3 minutes), 30 maximum steps, data entirely synthetic created by setup script. Key data values: subscription fee $9.99/month, meal kit waste 5-8%, grocery waste 18-35%. Expected cost comparisons: meal kit average $7.54/serving (including subscription), grocery average $7.04/serving (waste-adjusted), making meal kits approximately 7% more expensive. Task requires 7 specific criteria to be met (per-serving formulas, waste adjustments, averages, subscription integration, cost differences, reasonable values, proper formatting) with 75% pass threshold (≥5 of 7 criteria). All data values and formulas have been verified against setup_task.sh CSV data and README specifications.
